daily_load_assessment: Yesterday was a moderate double-session day resulting in 71 load points over 2.32 hours of cycling. While you did not log a high-intensity 'quality' session, your legs carry residual fatigue. TSB is calculated at -12.91, indicating you are safely in a productive loading zone. There are no subjective sleep or HRV limits reported, so we assume full physical readiness. However, under my strict coaching parameters, you have no room for junk volume today; you will either train with precise high-intensity execution or rest completely.
acute_status_assessment_3_weeks: Your 3-week training block (July 1 to July 21) shows 16 sessions with 784 training load points across 15.53 hours. This appears solid on paper, but a deeper dive reveals that 8 of these sessions were e-bike rides. E-biking is useful for active transport, but it does not provide the physiological stress needed to elevate your 5km running pace or your cycling FTP. With only 4 quality sessions in 21 days, you are under-stimulating your top-end systems. ATL is at 46.94 and CTL is at 34.03. You are building fitness, but the quality of this CTL is structurally weak due to excessive low-intensity commute volume.
sport_trend_assessment_6_months: Your 6-month sport trend shows a low baseline CTL of 34.03. While you have avoided injury and maintained consistency, your best FTP of 246W is below your target configuration of 250W. To drive your 5-minute VO2max and 20-minute FTP upward, we must transition you away from unstructured commuting and into structured intervals. Your running performance trend is currently stagnant because running sessions represent a minority of your recent workload. If you want a competitive 5km time, your running frequency must increase dramatically over the next 180 days.
coach_verdict: Today, we prioritize the RUNNING OPTION. Your 5km running goals are being neglected due to a high volume of cycling and commuting. Your TSB (-12.91) is far from your absolute limit of -30, meaning your body is fully capable of absorbing a high-intensity shock. Do not substitute this with an easy ride. If you choose to run, execute the intervals with strict pace discipline. If your legs feel heavy, do not slow down and turn it into a junk endurance run; stop the workout immediately.

cycling_option.workout_details: 15-minute warm-up including 3x30-sec fast-pedal efforts. Main set: 5x4 minutes at 285 Watts (114% of FTP, targeting 5-min power baseline) with 4 minutes of very easy spinning recovery between reps. 10-minute cool-down.
cycling_option.reason: This target session forces adaptations in stroke volume and anaerobic capacity, directly pushing your 5-minute power ceiling upward to lift your FTP.

running_option.workout_details: 10-minute progressive warm-up run + 3 dynamic drills. Main set: 5x1000m at your target 5km race pace (approx. 90-95% of max effort). Recovery: 2 minutes of complete walking rest between intervals. 10-minute easy jog cool-down.
running_option.reason: You need run-specific high-intensity volume. 1000m repeats build the specific aerobic power and psychological tolerance required to sustain your target pace over 5km.

recovery_option.workout_details: 30 to 45 minutes of flat, spin-only cycling on a standard bike or e-bike. Power must stay strictly below 125 Watts (under 50% FTP). Cadence kept high at 90-95 RPM.
recovery_option.reason: If your muscles feel highly restricted from yesterday's 2.32-hour ride, do not compromise your adaptation. Spin easily to promote blood flow and clear waste products without adding training load.
